title: Watch an asteroid the size of a blue whale hurtle towards Earth live online May 18
summary: An asteroid the size of a blue whale will be tracked live online on May 18, with a livestream starting at 3:45 p.m. EDT. The event will feature near real-time views from robotic telescopes in Italy, pending weather conditions.

title: Giant radio telescope sees Artemis 2 astronauts on Orion flying around the moon. 'There are 4 people in those pixels.'
summary: A giant radio telescope in West Virginia tracked Artemis 2's Orion spacecraft around the moon for five days, gathering precise observations on its movements during the historic mission. The telescope captured the astronauts' presence, with researchers noting, 'There are 4 people in those pixels.'

title: Get $341 off the limited X edition of the Vaonis Vespera II at Walmart
summary: Walmart is offering a $341 discount on the limited X edition of the Vaonis Vespera II telescope. The Vespera II X edition includes a transparent shell and internal temperature sensors for improved deep sky observation.

title: With the Vespera III and Vespera Pro 2, telescope-maker Vaonis unveils its sharpest optics yet
summary: Vaonis launched its new Vespera III and Vespera Pro 2 telescopes, featuring sharper optics and upgraded features for improved stargazing. These are the company's sharpest telescopes to date.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 210 — ESCAPADES at Mars
summary: Episode 210 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the Mars ESCAPADE mission with Dr. Robert Lillis. The episode covers the objectives and progress of the ESCAPADE mission to Mars.

title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is flying to a metal asteroid. So, why did it just visit Mars?
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft performed a flyby of Mars to utilize a gravity assist, which helps it gain speed and conserve fuel while traveling to a metal asteroid. This maneuver is a common technique in space missions to navigate the solar system efficiently.

title: Astronomers find interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS hiding in images taken before its official discovery
summary: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS was identified in archival images taken before its official discovery. The comet was almost named 3I/Rubin after being spotted by a survey telescope over a week prior.

title: The devil may wear Prada on Earth, but NASA Artemis astronauts will on the moon. Here's why.
summary: NASA's Artemis astronauts will wear Prada-designed spacesuits for the upcoming moon missions. The collaboration highlights the intersection of fashion and space technology.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments. The mission lifted off on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover successfully removed a stuck rock from its drill after days of troubleshooting. The rock had become lodged during a drilling operation on April 25, 2026.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The image shows reddish-brown dust filaments obscuring parts of the galaxy, with background light from distant galaxies visible.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis mapped changes in artificial light at night on Earth, highlighting areas where light pollution has increased or decreased. This study provides new insights into human activity and environmental impact from space.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) for the Mars Telecommunications Network, seeking industry collaboration to enable reliable, high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will use orbiters to support surface, orbital, and human exploration.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA and Eta Space are collaborating on the Liquid Oxygen Flight Demonstration (LOXSAT) to test cryogenic fuel management technologies for future deep space missions. The demo aims to enable in-space propellant depots, which could serve as 'gas stations' for long-term space exploration.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft delivered fresh food to the International Space Station, including oranges, apples, and onions, for NASA and ESA astronauts. The delivery was part of a resupply mission, showcasing ongoing support for long-duration space missions.

title: Ice Moves Out of Aniak
summary: Ice jams and flooding occurred along Alaska’s Kuskokwim River due to spring melt. The event highlights the impact of seasonal changes on river systems.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: A NASA-supported 3D printing innovation originally designed for lunar habitats is now being used by Branch Technology Inc. to create efficient and strong building structures on Earth.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: NASA's Artemis II zero gravity indicator 'Rise' was displayed during a congressional meeting in Washington, where astronauts met with staff to discuss the mission. The nearly 10-day Artemis II mission included astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The spacecraft has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. This expansion follows its strong performance in 2025.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its CRS-34 mission to the ISS on the third attempt. The mission follows the docking of Progress MS-34 to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China have a busy launch schedule with nine launches planned across the United States and China during the week of May 11. The launches span various missions, highlighting a significant period for both countries' space activities.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event was reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making progress toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The upgrade follows the completion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before a busier phase in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying the CAS500-2 satellite and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the news snippet, but no specific details or context are provided about its content or relevance to the space industry.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses the importance of sensitivity and sturdiness in space industry components. It highlights the need for robust and precise technology in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or bake-out procedure is mentioned. The specific details are not provided in the given text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed to securely grasp and manipulate objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes in-person and online sessions. The event will take place at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with preparatory online forums in April and May.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The deal aims to provide integrated and resilient deep-space communication solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This acquisition aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are competing for infrastructure financing as the region faces a growth-stage capital shortage. This highlights challenges in scaling space ventures within Europe's investment ecosystem.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program, stating it does not accurately reflect what is being built. The criticism highlights discrepancies between official projections and actual program costs.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company along with mentorship and support for defense market entry and fundraising.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined a preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test technologies for a subsequent Moon landing in 2028.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking its entry into the space industry. This milestone positions LEAP as a new competitor in the launch sector.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by Gen. Michael Guetlein in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device on the ISS. The automated mini lab is designed to fit into standard mid-deck lockers aboard crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff but left after Jared Isaacman became Administrator. His new role has drawn attention due to its political implications.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program disputed a Congressional Budget Office estimate regarding the cost of a system similar to one proposed during President Donald Trump's administration. The disagreement highlights differing perspectives on the financial implications of the program.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: The IFC market in China and India is expected to grow significantly according to Valour Consultancy's latest report. Valour predicts substantial expansion in these markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to provide data center services from space, following a $275 million Series B funding round. The company, previously rebranded, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data processing.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence. The capability was delivered in less than 12 months.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three largest US mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to collaborate on direct-to-device (D2D) services, aiming to enhance competition in the sector.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines has acquired Goonhilly Earth Station, a U.K.-based provider of deep space communication services. This acquisition aims to enhance Intuitive Machines' deep space communications capabilities.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is set to fully acquire Aireon, a company operating a space-based air traffic surveillance system using payloads on Iridium's network. The acquisition highlights opportunities in space-based VHF (Very High Frequency) technology.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues for the three months ending March, but saw significant growth in its space services revenue.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's low-cost Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.
